title: Thelypteris tildeniae
taxonomy: Thelypteris tildeniae is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Thelypteris is part of the larger group of ferns known as the wood ferns. These wood ferns are further classified as part of the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other fern species.
uses: Thelypteris tildeniae is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant's leaves are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties make it a potential candidate for the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
